note: No split (factor 1) -> nominal = raw close. DELTA EXPLANATION (~0.78%): the card as_of_price $111.00 is the StatMuse historical close (S005); Yahoo's META feed and the independent stockanalysis.com feed both report $111.87 for the 2022-11-10 close. The 0.778% gap is a CLOSE-SOURCE difference (StatMuse $111.00 vs the Yahoo/stockanalysis consensus $111.87) -- single-share-class stock, so no class ambiguity. The card's $111.00 reconciles EXACTLY with the reported market cap (2,651,548,674 shares from the 10-Q cover x $111.00 = $294.3B = card), confirming it as the intended nominal close. Both candidate closes are <1% apart, so the cross-check PASSes. Card NOT changed.

live_fields.low_high_hug_reason: Not hugging an extreme: $111.00 is -68.6% off the trailing-52wk high ($353.83) and +26.0% above the 52wk low ($88.09); the stock had bounced off its Oct-27 multi-year trough ($97.94) on the Nov-9 layoff news and the Nov-10 CPI rally, so it sits well inside the band, not at an edge. No justification required.

live_fields.value: As of 2022-11-10: the live forward signal is the Nov-9 cost pivot (S003, SEC-filed layoff message) -- first-ever layoff of ~11,000 (13% of staff) + hiring freeze + a commitment to a 'more efficient cost structure than we outlined recently', bending the Oct-26 FY2023 outlook of total expenses $96-101B and capex $34-39B. Reality Labs operating loss $(3,672)M in Q3, guided to grow in 2023. Nothing after 2022-11-10 (e.g. the 2023 cost-cut execution) is used.

sources.note: SEC-filed layoff/cost-discipline message, 1 day before as_of
